query: recent studies on vitiligo and metabolic syndrome

retrieved_docs.title: Vitiligo -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Vitiligo is a common acquired skin disorder that results from the loss of melanocytes from the epidermis and clinically manifests as well-demarcated white patches on the body. There are different theories about the pathogenesis of vitiligo, but the exact etiology is still unknown. It is a form of autoimmune disorder. This activity reviews the role of the interprofessional team in the diagnosis and treatment of this condition.

retrieved_docs.title: Vitiligo -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Describe the pathophysiology of vitiligo. Identify the clinical features of the various clinical types of vitiligo. Explain the various treatment options available for vitiligo. Outline the importance of collaboration and communication among the interprofessional team to ensure the appropriate treatment for patients with vitiligo. Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.

retrieved_docs.title: Vitiligo -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: A multidisciplinary approach is important because vitiligo is associated with other autoimmune disorders. Proper collaboration and communication are very important. These patients present with multiple psychological disorders due to their appearance and unpredictable course. Psychological interventions are important. Cognitive-behavioral therapy, along with regular conventional treatment, is very useful in improving the self-esteem, confidence, and quality of life of such patients, and it also influences the disease course.

retrieved_docs.title: Vitiligo -- Consultations
retrieved_docs.content: An interprofessional approach is essential for such patients. Sometimes, these patients need special referrals to respective specialties when presented with another autoimmune disorder. In addition, for ocular complications, consultation with an ophthalmologist and psychological ailments referral to mental health specialists is important.

retrieved_docs.title: Biochemistry, Melanin -- Clinical Significance -- Disorders of Melanin Synthesis and Transport
retrieved_docs.content: Vogt-Koyanagi-Harada syndrome is a multisystem autoimmune disease, with phases including: Meningoencephalitis Bilateral uveitis Alopecia and vitiligo-like depigmentation Recurrent ocular inflammation This syndrome is believed to result from autoimmune targeting of melanocyte-associated antigens, including tyrosinase. Neuromelanin and the central nervous system: Parkinson disease is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder marked by: Resting tremor, bradykinesia, rigidity, and postural instability Depigmentation of the substantia nigra pars compacta, reflecting the loss of neuromelanin-producing dopaminergic neurons This pigment loss is a pathological hallmark of the disease and correlates with motor symptoms.

retrieved_docs.title: Polyglandular Autoimmune Syndrome Type I -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: Vitiligo may be the initial manifestation and is a clinical feature in 33% of patients with APS-1. The degree of skin involvement is variable, and it can manifest as isolated spots or involving the whole skin. It correlates with the antibodies against SOX9 and SOX10. Unfortunately, there is no effective therapy against vitiligo. [9]

retrieved_docs.title: Vitiligo -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: The exact etiology of vitiligo is unknown. It is frequently associated with multiple autoimmune diseases. There are various theories about its pathogenesis and the etiology is multifactorial. It is characterized by incomplete penetrance, genetic heterogeneity, and multiple susceptibility loci. Family and other twin studies have shown that inheritance is complex and also involves both environmental and genetic factors. Additionally, it is hypothesized that genetic factors can influence the age of onset of vitiligo. The inheritance of vitiligo may also include genes associated with the biosynthesis of melanin, regulation of autoantibodies, and response to oxidative stress. [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Vitiligo -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: In the neural hypothesis, a neurochemical mediator usually destroys the melanocytes and decreases the production of melanin. In oxidant and anti-oxidant mechanisms, intermediate or metabolic products of melanin synthesis cause the destruction of the melanocyte. [7] In the intrinsic defect of melanocytes, there is an inherent abnormality that impedes their growth and differentiation. Another hypothesis is autoimmune or cytotoxic, where there is an alteration in humoral and cellular immunity that causes the destruction or dysfunction of melanocytes. This theory supports the hypothesis that nonsegmental vitiligo is more commonly associated with autoimmune disorders than the segmental type of vitiligo.

retrieved_docs.title: Vitiligo -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: Vitiligo is commonly known as a multifactorial polygenic disorder and has a complex pathogenesis. It is commonly associated with both non-genetic and genetic factors. However, various theories have been proposed about its pathogenesis, but the exact etiology is still unknown. The generally agreed principle is the absence of melanocytes in vitiligo skin with melanocyte loss, owing to their destruction. The destruction most results in progressive melanocyte decreases. Theories about melanocyte destruction include cytotoxic mechanisms, autoimmune mechanisms, intrinsic melanocyte defects, neural mechanisms, and oxidant-antioxidant mechanisms. [5]

retrieved_docs.title: Vitiligo -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: Progressive macular hypomelanosis: It is clinically present as asymptomatic hypopigmented patches on the trunk of young adults

retrieved_docs.title: Hypopigmented Macules -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: Autoimmune causes- Vitiligo, hypopigmented sarcoidosis

retrieved_docs.title: Stiff Person Syndrome -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Classic SPS is the most common clinical form, present in 70 to 80% of SPS patients. It is associated with anti-glutamic acid decarboxylase (anti-GAD) antibodies. [2] The condition has an insidious onset with gradual worsening over time and often leads to permanent disability and, in some cases, mortality. SPS may coexist with other autoimmune disorders, including  Diabetes Mellitus Type 1 (DM-1), autoimmune thyroid disease, pernicious anemia, celiac disease, vitiligo. [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Ginkgo Biloba -- Indications -- Vitiligo
retrieved_docs.content: A double-blind placebo-controlled trial of 52 vitiligo patients showed that treatment with Ginkgo biloba correlated with a statistically significant cessation of active progression of depigmentation. [29] Despite such a promising result, more studies are to validate ginkgo's role as a potential therapy of choice for vitiligo.

retrieved_docs.title: Vitiligo -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Vitiligo is an acquired pigmentary skin disorder caused by the absence of pigmentary cells from the epidermis that results in white macules and patches on the body. [1] The condition is usually associated with a few autoimmune disorders, with thyroid abnormalities being the most common one. The etiology of vitiligo is unknown but there are different theories to explain its pathogenesis. Vitiligo presents clinically with signs and symptoms of white spots on the body distributed symmetrically and more obvious in people with dark skin. The lesions are characterized by well-demarcated pearly white or depigmented macules and patches, oval, round, or linear-shaped, and the borders are convex, ranging from the size of a few millimeters to centimeters, and enlarged centrifugally. There are different clinical variants of vitiligo, which are trichrome, marginal inflammatory, and quadrichrome vitiligo. Koebner phenomenon (Development of vitiligo at specific trauma prone sites, like cut, burn, or abrasion)is also a common clinical manifestation. Initial lesions occur most frequently on the hands, forearms, feet, and face, favoring a periocular or perioral distribution. [2] [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Vitiligo -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: Recent research studies have not highlighted any associations with any certain HLA type. There is a strong reason to believe that segmental and nonsegmental vitiligo have a unique genetic mechanism, which can account for variable treatment responses. [5]

retrieved_docs.title: Vogt-Koyanagi-Harada Syndrome -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: The most common integumentary symptoms of VKH are poliosis and vitiligo. Poliosis and vitiligo have been shown to slow and respond well to systemic treatment with corticosteroids and IMT. Further, topical treatment with calcineurin inhibitors and corticosteroids has shown reversal of depigmented areas of skin. [81]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 1930
retrieved_docs.content: DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES is a broad classification that encompasses several distinct conditions. Within this category, مرض السكري دون مضاعفات refers to diabetes without complications, representing a form of the disease where hyperglycemia is present but secondary complications have not yet developed. فرط أنسولين الدم denotes hyperinsulinemia, a state of elevated insulin levels in the blood that can arise from pancreatic beta‑cell overactivity or insulin‑secreting tumors and may lead to hypoglycemia. ورم غلوكاغوني describes glucagonoma, a rare pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor that secretes excess glucagon, causing characteristic metabolic disturbances such as glucose intolerance, weight loss, and a distinctive skin rash. Together, these three entities illustrate the spectrum of disorders that fall under DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES, highlighting the varied clinical presentations and management considerations associated with pancreatic endocrine pathology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 3 -- Community 1452
retrieved_docs.content: DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES is a broad category that encompasses several specific conditions. Among its subentities are اعتلال عصبي سكري (diabetic neuropathy), a chronic complication in which prolonged hyperglycemia leads to damage of peripheral nerves and results in sensory loss, pain, or autonomic dysfunction. The category also includes فرط أنسولين الدم (hyperinsulinemia), a state of elevated insulin levels in the bloodstream that can arise from insulin resistance, early type 2 diabetes, or insulin‑secreting tumors and contributes to metabolic disturbances such as hypoglycemia and weight gain. Another child condition is ورم غلوكاغوني (glucagonoma), a rare neoplasm of the pancreatic α‑cells that secretes excess glucagon, producing a characteristic syndrome of hyperglycemia, necrolytic migratory erythema, weight loss, and anemia. Finally, the category lists ضمور نصف الوجه المترقي (progressive hemifacial atrophy), an uncommon, often autoimmune‑mediated disorder characterized by gradual wasting of the skin, subcutaneous tissue, and muscle on one side of the face; its inclusion reflects the recognition that endocrine pancreatic diseases, particularly those with autoimmune components, may be associated with extra‑pancreatic manifestations. Together, these entities illustrate the diverse clinical spectrum—from metabolic and neuropathic complications to neoplastic and autoimmune manifestations—linked to diseases of the endocrine pancreas, underscoring the importance of comprehensive evaluation and multidisciplinary management in affected patients.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 3 -- Community 1880
retrieved_docs.content: Disorders of lipid metabolism constitute a broad category of metabolic conditions affecting the processing and regulation of fats in the body, and hyperlipoproteinemia (فرط البروتينات الشحمية) is a specific disorder within this category. Hyperlipoproteinemia is characterized by abnormally high levels of lipoproteins in the blood, reflecting a particular manifestation of the underlying disturbances in lipid metabolism. Recognizing hyperlipoproteinemia as a subset of disorders of lipid metabolism underscores its role in the spectrum of lipid‑related diseases and informs targeted diagnostic and therapeutic approaches.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 3 -- Community 2282
retrieved_docs.content: DIABETES MELLITUS, TRANSIENT NEONATAL, 2 (disorder), Developmental Delay, Epilepsy, and Neonatal Diabetes, and Diabetes Mellitus, Transient Neonatal, 1 are all identified as specific manifestations or related entities of مرض السكري (diabetes). In clinical practice, مرض السكري is linked not only to its primary metabolic disturbances but also to other pancreatic disorders that are distinct from diabetes itself, highlighting the broader spectrum of pancreatic pathology that may coexist with or mimic diabetic presentations. Additionally, مرض السكري is associated with اضطراب في الشبكية (retinal disorder), reflecting the well‑known risk of diabetic retinopathy and other vision‑related complications in patients with diabetes. Furthermore, مرض السكري is recognized as having a child condition, متلازمة الأيض (metabolic syndrome), underscoring the interrelationship between diabetes and the cluster of metabolic risk factors that include obesity, hypertension, dyslipidemia, and insulin resistance. Together, these connections emphasize the heterogeneous nature of diabetes, its potential neonatal and developmental presentations, its association with other pancreatic and ocular disorders, and its role as a central component of metabolic syndrome, all of which are important considerations for comprehensive patient assessment and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 2404
retrieved_docs.content: Metabolic disorder (اضطراب التمثيل الغذائي) is a broad category of disease that includes carbohydrate metabolism disorder (اضطراب استقلاب الكربوهيدرات) as one of its subtypes. Within the hierarchy of carbohydrate metabolism disorder, other endocrine disorders are classified as a further subset, indicating that these endocrine conditions are considered specific forms of carbohydrate metabolism disorders. This hierarchical relationship highlights how metabolic disorders encompass a range of more specialized conditions, including those affecting carbohydrate processing and related endocrine functions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 3 -- Community 2046
retrieved_docs.content: DISEASES OF THE ENDOCRINE PANCREAS INCLUDING DIABETES encompasses a spectrum of related conditions. It includes ارتفاع السكر في الدم, which denotes elevated blood glucose levels, and مرض السكري دون مضاعفات, representing diabetes without complications. Neurological involvement is reflected by اعتلال عصبي سكري, a diabetic neuropathy. Facial manifestations may appear as ضمور نصف الوجه المترقي, a progressive hemi‑facial atrophy that can be associated with pancreatic endocrine disorders. Metabolic dysregulation is further illustrated by فرط أنسولين الدم, an excess of circulating insulin, and by ورم غلوكاغوني, a glucagon‑producing tumor of the pancreas. Together these entities illustrate the diverse clinical presentations and complications that arise from diseases of the endocrine pancreas, highlighting the importance of comprehensive assessment and management in affected patients.

retrieved_docs.content: Varicose veins of the lower extremity are commonly linked to the development of قَرحَة (skin ulcer), indicating that ulcerative lesions represent a typical morphological manifestation in patients with these venous disorders. retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 1740
retrieved_docs.content: Diagnosis/Diseases Component is classified under مرض السكري (diabetes mellitus). The specific disorders DIABETES MELLITUS, TRANSIENT NEONATAL, 2 (disorder) and Diabetes Mellitus, Transient Neonatal, 1 are both mapped to مرض السكري, indicating they are forms of transient neonatal diabetes that fall within the broader category of diabetes mellitus. The combined condition Developmental Delay, Epilepsy, and Neonatal Diabetes is also mapped to مرض السكري, highlighting its association with neonatal diabetes as part of the diabetes spectrum. Clinically, اضطراب في الشبكية (retinal disorder) is associated with مرض السكري, reflecting the well‑known ocular complications of diabetes. Additionally, متلازمة الأيض (metabolic syndrome) is identified as a child of مرض السكري, signifying that metabolic syndrome can arise as a downstream metabolic complication of diabetes. Together, these relationships illustrate how various neonatal diabetes subtypes, developmental and neurological manifestations, ocular complications, and metabolic syndrome are interconnected within the overarching framework of diabetes mellitus, underscoring the importance of comprehensive assessment and management in patients with diabetes.
